response:
a restaurant owner wants to transform their establishment into a tranquil oasis for diners. to achieve this, they plan to redesign the dining area and incorporate a small zen garden in the center. the dining area is currently a rectangular space measuring 20 meters in length and 15 meters in width.

the zen garden will be a circular area with a radius of r meters, and it will be placed exactly in the center of the dining area. the owner wants the total area available for dining tables to be twice the area of the zen garden. calculate the radius r of the zen garden. the area of the dining area is 20 * 15 = 300 square meters. the area of the zen garden is πr². the total area available for dining tables is 300 - πr². this area is twice the area of the zen garden, so 300 - πr² = 2πr². solving this equation gives r² = 300 / (3π) = 100 / π. thus, r = sqrt(100 / π) ≈ 5.64 meters.

the owner decides to install a small fountain at the center of the zen garden. the fountain will be a cylinder with a height of 1.5 meters and a radius that is one-third of the zen garden's radius. if the fountain is to be made of a material costing $50 per square meter, determine the total cost to cover the surface area of the cylindrical fountain (excluding the base area in contact with the zen garden). the surface area of the cylinder is 2πr * h, where r is the radius and h is the height. the radius of the fountain is r/3. substituting this into the formula gives 2π(r/3) * 1.5 = 2πr. the cost is 2πr * 50 = 100πr. substituting r = sqrt(100 / π) gives 100π * sqrt(100 / π) = 100π * 10 / sqrt(π) = 1000π / sqrt(π) = 1000 * sqrt(π). the total cost is approximately 1000 * 1.772 = 1772 dollars.
